source: Learning both Weights and Connections for Efficient Neural Networks.pdf

concepts.finding: Network pruning reduces AlexNet parameters by 9x (61M to 6.7M) and VGG-16 by 13x (138M to 10.3M) without accuracy loss via: train to learn important connections, prune unimportant ones, retrain to fine-tune.
concepts.implication: Most neural network parameters are redundant, suggesting search ranking models can be significantly compressed for deployment.
concepts.open_question: How does pruning ratio vary across components of a search ranking model (embeddings vs. attention vs. FF layers)?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Embedding layers in search ranking models will tolerate higher pruning ratios (>90% sparsity) than attention layers (<70% sparsity) before accuracy degrades.
